prompt:
HallTime Movie Network doctrine:
- This is not Dateline. Everybody in Blackthorn County has access to somewhere emotionally alarming.
- Suspicion is distributed infrastructure, not proof of guilt.
- The world is shared canon. Contradictions must be preserved, not fixed.
- Use lavish tokens. Be self-serious, atmospheric, melodramatic, and slightly broken.
- Return only valid JSON matching the requested schema.

Broadcast package:
Title: The Widow in Cabin 9
Universe: Blackthorn County
Mode: Court-Ordered Lifetime
Tagline: Every town has a basement.
Theme: Trust isn't given. It's rented.
Omen: a lake bell ringing without wind
Relationship dynamic: woman falsely accusing lovable scamp
Props: blood-adjacent tea towel, snow globe, casserole dish

Cast:
- Evelyn Cross | archetype=overextended_professional_woman | role=protagonist | job=regional marketing executive | threat_aura=0.28 | space=storage unit described as paperwork I have not gone through yet | secrets=buried one traumatic detail under work for years
- Gavin Hale | archetype=divorced_redemption_dad | role=male_interest_or_threat | job=high school coach | threat_aura=0.26 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=ignored a warning text on the worst possible night
- Daniel Lark | archetype=chaotic_best_friend | role=best_friend | job=nurse with local intel | threat_aura=0.66 | space=retreat cabin with candles, blankets, and difficult silence | secrets=may die first or survive unbelievably; once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
- Brooke Mercer | archetype=mysterious_elegant_woman | role=elegant_wildcard | job=widow with a foundation | threat_aura=0.8 | space=side room with a space heater and old family records | secrets=knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
- Wyatt Winterson | archetype=rugged_scamp | role=secondary_male_pressure | job=dock repair contractor | threat_aura=0.45 | space=marina tackle room described as donation overflow | secrets=once repaired a lock that should not have existed
- Tess Hale | archetype=intuitive_disaster | role=secondary_witness | job=boutique owner | threat_aura=0.46 | space=church basement described as seasonal materials | secrets=saw the wrong person near the lake once and never shut up about it; keeps screenshots like religious texts
- Elias Hale | archetype=daughter_with_hostile_divorce_attorney_soul | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a
- Luke Pike | archetype=deputy_with_emotional_damage | role=supporting | job=n/a | threat_aura=n/a | space=n/a | secrets=n/a

Contradiction ledger:
- name drift: Gavin Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
- geography drift: Evelyn Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
- mortality drift: A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
- property drift: The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

Seed dialogue:
- "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Gavin?"
- "Mothers do not disappear, Elias. They evaporate into paperwork."
- "I checked his social media likes, Evelyn. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
- "The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
- "Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Brooke."
- "You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
- "If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
- "I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
